sections.paragraph:
ἑτερογνώμων, ον, gen. ονος (s. prec. entry; Vett. Val. 79, 18; Agathias 4, 2.—ἑτερογνωμοσύνη; Jos., Ant. 10, 281) of a different opinion γυναικὸς ἑτερογνώμονος ὑπαρχούσης the woman (Lot’s wife) who was of a different opinion 1 Cl 11:2.—M-M. s.v. ἑτερόγλωσσος.

gen. gen. = genitive

prec. prec. = preceding

Agathias Agathias , VI a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 398
